summary: The author achieved 4th place in the Kaggle Jigsaw competition by applying SALSA (Single-pass Autoregressive LLM Structured Classification), which treats next-token logits from an instruction-tuned LLM as a Bayesian estimator for classification. They replaced standard cross-entropy with Generalized Cross-Entropy (Tsallis) to handle label noise and implemented a two-stage training pipeline using pseudo-labeling on uncertain test samples to boost performance. Key technical choices included removing the subreddit field, collapsing duplicates to compute soft targets, and optimizing inference via KV-cache reuse through strategic prompt templating and batching. The final submission blended predictions from a 4B and 14B Qwen-3 model to achieve a private AUC of 0.9286.
extensive_summary:
## Overview & Core Methodology
The solution leverages **SALSA** (Single-pass Autoregressive LLM Structured Classification), a framework that treats an instruction-tuned LLM's next-token logits over a constrained vocabulary as a Bayesian estimator for classification. Instead of multi-step decoding, the method relies on a single pass where normalized logits directly map to class probabilities. This approach capitalizes on the strong zero-shot baselines of larger, well-aligned instruction models.

## Data Processing & Preparation
The author made several critical data modifications to improve signal quality. The **subreddit** field was removed because it introduced misleading signals unrelated to the few-shot examples. Training duplicates were collapsed into unique texts, and a **rule-violation ratio** was computed per unique text-comment pair to serve as a **soft target** during training. Additionally, each training record was augmented with four few-shot exemplars (two positive, two negative) to act as auxiliary labels.

## Training & Loss Optimization
To mitigate label noise, standard cross-entropy was replaced with **Generalized Cross-Entropy (Tsallis)** with `q = 0.955`, which provided a measurable boost to the public leaderboard score. The author experimented with **Qwen-3 4B** and **Qwen-3 14B** backbones, utilizing **AWQ** quantization to preserve model capability within Kaggle's compute constraints. Inference was optimized by placing the comment at the end of the prompt template to maximize prefix KV-cache reuse, and by batching inputs by rule.

## Two-Stage Pseudo-Labeling & Ensembling
A two-stage pipeline was introduced to extract additional AUC without increasing compute. A smaller model generated predictions on the test set, and ~8,000 highly uncertain samples were selected as soft pseudo-labels. The larger Qwen-3 14B model was then finetuned on the original data combined with these pseudo-labels. The final submission blended Stage-1 and Stage-2 predictions with an 0.8:0.2 weight ratio, yielding a private AUC of 0.9286. The author notes that while the gain was decisive for ranking, the added engineering complexity requires careful cost-benefit analysis.
competition_flow: Raw text data was cleaned by removing the subreddit field and collapsing duplicates to create soft targets, then fed into a two-stage SALSA pipeline using instruction-tuned Qwen-3 models with pseudo-labeling and KV-cache optimized inference to produce blended classification predictions.
data_processing: Removed subreddit field due to misleading signals
Collapsed training duplicates to create unique dataset
Computed rule-violation ratio per unique text to generate soft targets
Added four few-shot exemplars (2 positive, 2 negative) per record as auxiliary labels
ensembling: Blended predictions from the Stage-1 (small model) and Stage-2 (large model with pseudo-labels) pipelines using a 0.8:0.2 weight ratio.
insights: Treating next-token logits over a constrained vocabulary as a Bayesian estimator allows direct probability mapping without multi-step decoding.
Larger and better-aligned instruction models yield stronger SALSA signals due to superior zero-shot baselines.
Pseudo-labeling uncertain test samples anchors relative scores rather than introducing new knowledge, effectively leveraging pretrained model capabilities.
The engineering complexity of a two-stage pseudo-labeling pipeline must be weighed against its modest AUC gains relative to compute budget.
critical_findings: Dataloader seed changes caused ~0.002 AUC variance, making smaller gains like those from Generalized Cross-Entropy statistically insignificant.
The subreddit field introduced misleading signals because the few-shot examples were unrelated to it.
Reordering the prompt template to place instructions at the end yielded negligible improvements for the smaller model, falling below statistical significance.

papers.title: SALSA: Single-pass Autoregressive LLM Structured Classification.
